download_details.title: BWRX-300 Small Modular Reactor | GE Vernova Hitachi Nuclear
GE Hitachi Nuclear Energy - BWRX-300 | Description & Characteristics

findings: The BWRX-300 has an electrical output capacity of 300 MWe and is a Boiling Water Reactor (BWR) small modular reactor (SMR) developed by GE Vernova Hitachi Nuclear Energy.

The BWRX-300 has a net electrical output of 300 MWe and a thermal output of 870 MWth, using water as both coolant and moderator, boiling in the core to produce steam via natural circulation without recirculation pumps.
The BWRX-300's technical parameters confirm an electric power output of 300 MWe, thermal power output of 870 MWth, H2O as both coolant and neutron moderator, and reactor type classified as BWR.
The BWRX-300 is a small modular reactor with an electrical capacity of 300 MWe, using naturally circulating water cooling where pumps are not required for reactor safety.
The BWRX-300 is a smaller evolution of the ESBWR and utilizes components of the operational ABWR; boiling water reactors use ordinary light water as a nuclear reactor coolant.
